Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.terminal

Class Terminal

java.lang.Object
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.terminal.Terminal
public final class Terminal extends Object
The terminal associated with the JVM.

  • Constructor Details

    • Terminal

      public Terminal(NativeTerminal nativeTerminal)
      Parameters:
      nativeTerminal - the native terminal (null if unavailable)

  • Method Details

    • getSupportedTypes

      public Set<TerminalEncoding> getSupportedTypes()
      Returns:
      the ANSI escape codes supported by the terminal

    • setEncoding

      public void setEncoding(TerminalEncoding encoding)
      Indicates the type of encoding that the terminal should use.

      This feature can be used to force the use of ANSI colors even when their support is not detected.

      Parameters:
      encoding - the type of encoding that the terminal should use
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if encoding is null
      See Also:
      • useBestEncoding()

    • useBestEncoding

      public void useBestEncoding()
      Indicates that verifiers should output the best encoding supported by the terminal.
      See Also:
      • setEncoding(TerminalEncoding)

    • getEncoding

      public TerminalEncoding getEncoding()
      Returns:
      the encoding that the terminal should use (defaults to the best available encoding)

    • isConnectedToStdout

      public boolean isConnectedToStdout()
      Returns:
      true if stdout is connected to the terminal

    • setWidth

      public void setWidth(int width)
      Indicates the width that the terminal should use.

      This feature can be used to override the default terminal width when it cannot be auto-detected.

      Parameters:
      width - the width that the terminal should use
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if width is zero or negative
      See Also:
      • useBestWidth()

    • useBestWidth

      public void useBestWidth()
      Indicates that verifiers should use the best width supported by the terminal. If the width cannot be auto-detected, a value of 80 is used.
      See Also:
      • setWidth(int)

    • getWidth

      public int getWidth()
      Returns:
      the width of the terminal in characters (defaults to the auto-detected width)
